title: eriogonum deserticola
intro: Eriogonum deserticola, also known as Mormon tea, is a shrubby perennial plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to act as a diuretic, help lower blood pressure, and soothe respiratory issues due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant has been used to aid in the treatment of various health conditions, including digestive issues, fever, and sore throats, with its health benefits attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids, alkaloids, and other bioactive compounds. Eriogonum deserticola can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties and reap its health benefits.
